name: Azure Service Bus Python SDK

auth.notes: Connection string, SAS token, or azure-identity credential

performance.notes: AMQP protocol; batching up to 256KB per message; 80GB session state

agent_readiness.notes: Enterprise message broker for Azure. from azure.servicebus import ServiceBusClient, ServiceBusMessage. with ServiceBusClient.from_connection_string(conn_str) as client: with client.get_queue_sender('myqueue') as sender: sender.send_messages(ServiceBusMessage('Hello')). Receive: with client.get_queue_receiver('myqueue') as receiver: for msg in receiver: process(msg); receiver.complete_message(msg). Topics/subscriptions for pub/sub. Sessions for ordered delivery. Dead-letter queues. Message scheduling. Lock renewal for long processing. Async support: ServiceBusClient(conn_str). Max message size 256KB (100MB premium).

gotchas: Lock expiry during long processing requires lock renewal (extend lock manually)
Dead-letter queue must be checked separately for failed messages
Topics require Standard or Premium tier (not Basic)
AMQP-based — not simple HTTP like SQS
best_when: Azure enterprise message queuing with guaranteed delivery
Pub/sub patterns via topics and subscriptions
Workflow orchestration requiring dead-letter queues and scheduled messages
avoid_when: High-throughput event streaming (use Azure Event Hubs instead)
Simple task queues without ordering/delivery guarantees (Storage Queues cheaper)
summary: Azure Service Bus Python SDK for enterprise messaging. ServiceBusClient.from_connection_string(conn_str). Queue sender/receiver with complete_message() for guaranteed delivery. Topics/subscriptions for pub/sub. Sessions for ordered delivery. Dead-letter queues, message scheduling, lock renewal. AMQP protocol. Async support. Best for enterprise Azure workflows requiring guaranteed delivery, ordering, and dead-letter handling. Use Event Hubs for high-throughput event streaming.
